caiso.Project Name: Julian Hinds - Mirage 230 kV Advanced Reconductor Project
caiso.Description: Contingency analysis indicates that the updated line ratings would effectively mitigate the thermal overloads without the need to activate the Blythe RAS. Serving as a vital corridor for solar and battery storage in Riverside County, this project will utilize ACCC® conductors to increase ampacity and reduce energy losses. These upgrade costs are expected to be partially subsidized by the U.S. Department of Energy GRIP grant funding awarded through the CHARGE 2T project.

caiso.Project Name: Reconductor of GWF – Kingsburg 115 kV line
caiso.Description: Mitigate overloads identified in the on-peak baseline deliverability study and support SB 100 clean energy goals. Likely to utilize high-capacity advanced conductors, such as CTC Global's ACCC®, for increased efficiency. This would need to update the limiting components at the substations if there is any. Protection may also need to be upgraded.

Reconductor Eagle Rock-020/087A and update any limiting components at the substation (may require relay upgrades). These upgrades will support the integration of renewable resources and contribute to California's ambitious SB 100 clean energy goals.

Reconductor 020/87A-037/191A and update any limiting components at the substation (if any). These upgrades will support the integration of renewable resources and contribute to California's ambitious SB 100 clean energy goals.

caiso.Project Name: El Patio – San Jose A 115 kV Line Reconductor
caiso.Description: The set of 115 kV reinforcements proposed in this project, along with the major 500 kV  transmission new project for the Bay Area and the re-scoping of two other projects in the San  Jose area, will provide sufficient transmission capacity to meet long-term load growth needs.

caiso.Description: The set of 115 kV reinforcements proposed in this project, along with the major 500 kV  transmission new project for the Bay Area and the re-scoping of two other projects in the San  Jose area, will provide sufficient transmission capacity to meet long-term load growth needs.

caiso.Project Name: Whisman – Monta Vista 115 kV Line Reconductor
caiso.Description: The set of 115 kV reinforcements proposed in this project, along with the major 500 kV  transmission new project for the Bay Area and the re-scoping of two other projects in the San  Jose area, will provide sufficient transmission capacity to meet long-term load growth needs.

The project originally proposed to reconductor the following 115 kV lines to 795 ACSS  conductors or an equivalent: Piercy-Metcalf, Swift-Metcalf, and Newark-Dixon Landing. Project was  approved in the 2003 Transmission Planning Process (TPP), was intended to alleviate these  long-term overloads. However, due to significant increases in the load forecast, as cited in  previous projects, and the additional load growth expectation in the San Jose area has 
motivated new transmission projects for the area and the re-scoping of existing ones.

caiso.Project Name: Tesla - Newark 230 kV Line No. 2 Reconductoring
caiso.Description: To mitigate overloads identified as part of Interconnection Alternative E, the ISO recommends approval.
Expansion of previously approved project Tulucay-Napa #2 60 kV Line Capacity Increase to include line reconductoring.

caiso.Project Name: TL695B Japanese Mesa Talega Tap Reconductor
caiso.Description: Eliminate NERC Category B violation to increase reliability by reconductoring TL 695 and TL 6971.

caiso.Project Name: Rio Oso - W. Sacramento Reconductoring Project
caiso.Description: Reconductoring Rio Oso – W. Sacramento 115 kV line as original re-rate of this  line as a part of Vaca Dixon reinforcement project approved in 2017-18 TPP is no  longer viable due to aging infrastructure.

caiso.Project Name: Reconductor Rio Oso–SPI Jct–Lincoln 115 kV line
caiso.Description: he new conductor will eliminate forecasted line overloads.  PG&E proposes to replace wooden power poles with a combination of concrete, wood, and tubular steel poles (TSP) within the existing power line corridor.
Addressing the near-term overloads on the Vaca-Plainfield 60 kV line resulting from NERC Category P0 and P1 contingencies.
The project will address thermal overloads in the Fresno area.
To mitigate P1 overloads identified as part of Interconnection Alternative E, the ISO is recommending approval. It will  take an estimated 24 months to complete.
Rebuild/build Kramer–Victor 115 kV lines to 230 kV and Loop the old segment of Kramer–Victor 115 kV into Roadway

The project proposes to reinforce 45 circuit miles of the 230 kV transmission lines between Bellota, Cottle, and Warnerville substations with 1113 kcmil steel-supported aluminum conductors with emergency capability of 1890 Amps. The project includes the installation of a new Optical Ground Wire (OPGW) that will replace the existing aging third-party owned OPGW.

caiso.Project Name: Midway – Kern PP 230 kV Line (Bakersfield-Kern Reconductor)
caiso.Description: PG&E proposes to raise approximately half of the existing towers along the line (approximately 60) approximately 7 to 27 feet by adding new extensions to the towers. Minor foundation upgrades and one foundation replacement will be completed on these towers as needed. The tower raises are required to support the new conductors, meet current design standards, reduce electric and magnetic fields near residences, and comply with minimum ground-to-conductor clearance requirements.

Q1-2024: This project is part of a multi-phased process to rebuild/reconductor in the Ripon-Manteca area. Long lead materials expected in Q1 2026. Permit completion is expected in Q1 2026. Project is soliciting bids for the design contract. Reconductor Ripon-Manteca 115 kV line 1.51 mi section of 2/0 CU and 0.92 mi 4/0 ACSR (2.43 mi total)

caiso.Project Name: Midway-Temblor 115 kV Line Reconductor and Voltage Support

caiso.Description: Category P2 overloads identified on sections of the Herndon-Bullard # 1 & # 2 lines. The P2 overloads are due to loss of either of the two parallel circuits from Herndon to Pinedale Junction resulting in one of the feeds serving the entire load at Bullard and Pinedale substation.
Reconductor the Christie - Sobrante 115 kV line with a larger capacity conductor

RAS was considered as an alternative but was not selected due to not meeting the RAS guiedlines. Series compensation was also considered as an alternative but was not selected due to the size that would be needed to mitigate the overload
Following a re-scope of the project to replace aging conductors with upgraded lines. It aims to support regional reliability and manage increasing electrical demand in Merced County, California.

This project aims to provide a comprehensive solution to address the high demand growth in South Oakland by upgrading five 115 kV lines which serve as the primary source in this pocket. By increasing transmission capacity for serving South Oakland Pocket, this project will ensure long-term reliability of the grid and its ability to serve the growing needs.
